dealKiller: The May 7, 2026 EU political agreement on the Digital AI Omnibus pushed high-risk AI obligations from Aug 2, 2026 to Dec 2, 2027 (standalone) and Aug 2, 2028 (embedded-in-product). The candidate's entire Year-1 thesis ('Aug-2026 AI Act deadlines force action; family-tier skips procurement') was written exactly as the deadline that powers it was being deleted. The forcing function collapses 14-26 months out, which is precisely the discovery-to-renewal window where this services firm needs urgency-driven sole-source procurement. Without the August 2026 cliff, Greek industrial mid-caps revert to default behavior: wait, watch peers, defer until Q3-2027 — past the Phase-0 kill date and into the year where Big 4 amortize their AI-Act productized assets and platforms (OneTrust AI Governance, Modulos) hit the Greek market via Big-4 resale at sub-€100K bundle pricing.
patternMatches: Regulatory-deadline-driven services thesis where the regulator moves the deadline mid-build (classic 'compliance cliff' arbitrage failure mode — same pattern killed NIS2 boutiques in H2-2024)
Buyer list is name-checked, not interviewed: no public evidence Mytilineos/Titan/Viohalco/Fourlis/Quest are actually deploying Annex-III scope AI systems in 2025-26; trigger volume is theoretical

Greek consulting price-floor claim ('Big 4 floor €200K') is unverified — Greek market is 40-60% below London/Brussels and Karatzas/Bernitsas/Zepos under-quote Big 4 on regulated-services dossiers as a standing pattern

Quest Holdings DORA scope is on its financial-services arm which almost certainly already retained EY or PwC Greece by Q1-2025 (DORA went live Jan 17, 2025; law 5193/2025 published April 11, 2025) — buyer has already chosen
HDPA + EDPB Support-Pool-of-Experts shipped free AI training material for DPOs in 2025 — same pattern that commoditized DPIAs 18 months after GDPR went live
Cross-sell hedge ('DORA + CSRD carries it') is hand-waved — DORA buyers bought 12-16 months ago; CSRD digital tagging is an existing Big-4 audit-adjacency win
unitEconomicsConcern: €90K ACV × 6-8 clients assumes 6-8 wins from a named list of 5 buyers — coverage ratio >100% with zero margin for loss. Realistic conversion at family-tier is 2-3 of 5 = €180-270K Year-1 cash, not €540-720K. Attorney retainer €8-12K/month = €96-144K fixed cost, plus €36K agent infra + Dimitri opportunity cost. Below 3 paid clients, contribution margin is negative. The Aug-2026 urgency was the only mechanism delivering >50% conversion; without it, conversion drops to GDPR-era 20-30%.
loadBearingAssumption: That regulatory deadline urgency, not buyer pull or competitive positioning, drives the sale. With the deadline gone, the candidate has no differentiated wedge — Big 4 have AI-Act practice leads in Athens by now (Deloitte/EY actively recruiting through 2025-26), GRC platforms ship by mid-2027, and Karatzas/Bernitsas absorb the legal-opinion layer at €30-50K standalone. 'Greek attorney countersigns AI-drafted dossier' is also untested under DSA ethics rules — Hellenic Bar has issued no positive opinion on this workflow as of May 2026.
alternativeThesis: Pivot to candidate #3 (Family-Office Investment Memos) — higher p=0.55, founder-fit 8.5/10, no regulatory-deadline dependency, trust-based moat that Big 4 structurally cannot replicate, and Dimitri already does this informally on T-Life capital. If regulatory-services is the must-have shape, narrow to DORA-only retention work for the 8-12 Greek non-bank financial entities (asset managers, payment institutions, crypto-asset service providers) where (a) the regulation is already in force, (b) Big 4 underserve the sub-€500M-AUM tier, and (c) Bank of Greece + HCMC supervisory pressure is escalating — but expect €40-60K ACV ceiling and 4-5 clients max = €200-300K, which is below the Dimitri-runnable threshold.
